fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 32-year-old White male with schizophrenia for medication check.
fields.Subjective: Patient arrived appearing calm and cooperative. Reports taking his aripiprazole 10 mg every morning consistently using phone reminder app. States doing well. No hallucinations for four months. No paranoid thoughts. Sleep good at 7 hours nightly. Appetite healthy, weight stable at 169 lbs. Working part-time at bookstore, enjoys helping customers find books. Mood 'good' rated 7/10. Lives independently in studio apartment. Enjoys reading science fiction and writing short stories. Attends creative writing group weekly. One year sober from alcohol. Sister visits monthly. Building new friendships through writing group. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Amanda Clark, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, good grooming, calm demeanor

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC and CMP within normal limits.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Living independently; working part-time; one year sober; engaged in creative pursuits and social activities.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence and symptom control.
fields.Author: Note written by Amanda Clark, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Booklovers Mental Health, 1567 Library Street, Portland, OR 97214, Phone: (555) 661-3398, Fax: (555) 661-3399
